text: We initiate coverage of Dataflow Analytics with a Strong Buy rating and a 12-month price target of $778. Our conviction is underpinned by robust emerging market demand and a structurally improving strong free cash flow generation backdrop. Management has demonstrated consistent execution against stated KPIs, and we view the current valuation as an attractive entry point relative to peers in the Energy space. From a valuation standpoint, Dataflow Analytics trades at 28x forward earnings, a 24% discount to the Energy peer group median.

From a valuation standpoint, Dataflow Analytics trades at 28x forward earnings, a 24% discount to the Energy peer group median. We use a blended DCF and EV/EBITDA methodology to arrive at our $778 price target, assuming a 10% WACC and terminal growth rate of 2.9%. Sensitivity analysis suggests the stock remains attractive even under our bear case assumptions of 8% revenue growth. In the North America context, Energy dynamics have shifted meaningfully over the past 18 months.

In the North America context, Energy dynamics have shifted meaningfully over the past 18 months. Robust emerging market demand has emerged as the primary growth catalyst, with management guiding for 25% year-over-year revenue acceleration in the back half of FY1999. We note that strong free cash flow generation provides an additional option value not reflected in consensus estimates. Our channel checks corroborate management's commentary on improving demand trends across North America end markets. Key risks to our Strong Buy thesis include geopolitical disruption in key markets and FX headwinds in emerging markets.

Key risks to our Strong Buy thesis include geopolitical disruption in key markets and FX headwinds in emerging markets. Additionally, near-term currency headwinds could weigh on near-term results and delay the expected re-rating. We would revisit our thesis on evidence of sustained margin deterioration below 22% gross margins or a material guidance cut at the next earnings event. Investors should be aware of the stock's beta of 1.19 relative to the broader market.
Investors should be aware of the stock's beta of 1.19 relative to the broader market.

We reiterate our Strong Buy rating on Nexora Systems following our annual deep-dive into Healthcare fundamentals across Middle East & Africa. favorable commodity price dynamics continues to drive above-consensus revenue growth, while margin expansion driven by a de-leveraging balance sheet supports our above-consensus EPS estimates for FY2010. Key risks include execution risk on new management strategy and credit market tightening. From a valuation standpoint, Nexora Systems trades at 26x forward earnings, a 22% discount to the Healthcare peer group median. We use a blended DCF and EV/EBITDA methodology to arrive at our $114 price target, assuming a 13% WACC and terminal growth rate of 1.7%. Sensitivity analysis suggests the stock remains attractive even under our bear case assumptions of 5% revenue growth.

Sensitivity analysis suggests the stock remains attractive even under our bear case assumptions of 5% revenue growth. In the Middle East & Africa context, Healthcare dynamics have shifted meaningfully over the past 18 months. Favorable commodity price dynamics has emerged as the primary growth catalyst, with management guiding for 28% year-over-year revenue acceleration in the back half of FY2010. We note that robust emerging market demand provides an additional option value not reflected in consensus estimates. Our channel checks corroborate management's commentary on improving demand trends across Middle East & Africa end markets.

Key risks to our Strong Buy thesis include execution risk on new management strategy and credit market tightening. Additionally, macro sensitivity to rate cycle could weigh on near-term results and delay the expected re-rating. We would revisit our thesis on evidence of sustained margin deterioration below 26% gross margins or a material guidance cut at the next earnings event.
